title: Past-filed context atoms violate rhetorical-register at scale — provenance/process/git narration in block bodies that compose verbatim into subagent contexts
description: A substrate-wide audit of all 17 block kinds found ~197 rhetorical-register violations across ~118 items. Dominant class: rule 5 (provenance / process / git narration), with rule 1 (declarative-not-narration) co-firing. Three sub-classes by volume: closure/outcome fields used as a changelog (closed_by / resolved_by / notes-at-completion — the largest source); origin/discovery narration; assert-then-refute and in-body corrections. Hedging and first-person are near-absent. The register-guarded block (framework-gaps) has the cleanest bodies, with a recency gradient — post-guard items clean, legacy FGAP-001..033 violating; tasks show the same gradient; unguarded blocks show none. session-notes is excluded: narrative is its purpose (rule 6). Two enforcement gaps: the register checkpoint guard covers only framework-gaps, and no pass cleans the already-filed violating bodies.

evidence.reference: Full audit: ~197 violations / ~118 items across 17 block kinds; per-block tallies (items audited, violating, by severity + by rule#), the violations table (block/id/field/rule/severity/offending quote), and the recency gradient.
35/35 verification items: the evidence field carries git-SHA + probe-loop process narration (rule 5).
~17/17 decisions: the context field carries origin narration (rule 5).
11/11 research items: findings_summary carries in-body corrections / assert-then-refute (rule 5).
2/2 issues: body + resolved_by carry discovery + git narration (rule 5).
closed_by on legacy gaps + bodies of FGAP-001..033 violate; post-guard gaps are clean — the gradient showing the checkpoint guard works where applied.

impact: Block bodies compose verbatim into subagent contexts — the substrate's stated purpose — so provenance/process/git narration is injected as operating context: noise that dilutes the atomic instruction and can mislead a downstream consumer. The violations concentrate in outcome/closure fields, the fields a consumer reads to learn current state.
canonical_vocabulary: rhetorical-register compliance across filed context atoms
proposed_resolution: Broaden the register checkpoint guard to the write-heavy blocks (tasks, verification, decisions, issues, research, features; exclude session-notes per rule 6), covering append AND closure/update writes, and clean every existing violating body to declarative current-truth with no signal loss. Tracked by the broadening + remediation task.
